sections.content:
The board didn't change after clicking on Box 4. The game seems to be stuck in a state where I can't make progress. Let me check the result of the last action to see if the game is still in a "game over" state.
Actually, looking at the current frame, it looks the same as before. The T-shape is still at the top-left, Box 1 is still selected, and the buttons are in the same state.
I think the game is in a "game over" state where I can't interact with the gameplay elements. The only thing that changes is the HUD.
Let me try to find a way to restart the level. Maybe clicking on a specific element will restart the level.
Actually, let me reconsider the game mechanics. Looking at the current frame:
1. The T-shape is at the top-left of the left panel.
2. Box 1 is selected (blue border).
3. The Right H and V buttons are all ON.
4. The trigger is at the bottom-right.
The game over state was triggered by clicking on the left panel at (10, 10). This moved the T-shape and changed the selected box. But then the game went into a "game over" state.
Maybe the game over state is a "fail" state, and I need to restart the level by clicking on a specific element.
Let me try clicking on the top-left corner of the screen or some other element to see if it restarts the level.
Actually, I realize I should stop wasting actions and try to understand the game mechanics from the history. Let me look at the early history to see what actions were taken in levels 1 and 2.

for i, t in enumerate(transitions):

        print(f"  Transition {i}: action={t.action}, level_completed={t.result.get('level_completed')}, game_over={t.result.get('game_over')}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
